---- {{Collapse|num=back1|final=>Open attachment 01|body= ---- ==
The Truth Behind All illusion
==
Chapter I — The Illusion of Perseverance
My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep... My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work… '''Was today any different?''' Hello. My name is Stefan Kronecker, and I have been an [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]]. employee for over four years. I arrived in this realm in 1995, and I am currently stationed in a small settlement within [[Level 4]]. The M.E.G. originally recruited me for the exploration of potentially dangerous levels, but years later I was transferred to the cybersecurity and database analysis division. My colleagues and I are responsible for the constant updating of the database, as well as the detection and removal of dangerous malware. Until a few months ago, that was my entire life. Everything began on January 10th, 1999. At the time, I assumed it would be just another day among countless monotonous and uneventful days and nights. My calendar indicated that I was scheduled to search for and purge unknown and potentially malicious files, so I sat down at my computer and began working. Four hours passed in the blink of an eye, and with them came exhaustion. I was about to take a break when I stumbled upon a file with a peculiar name. The file was titled '''“The Truth behind All illusiOn,”''' and it immediately caught my attention. It wasn’t that I hadn’t encountered oddly named files before—over the years I had come across countless files with strange or uncommon titles—but this one drew me in in an inexplicable way. The only way I can describe it is that it felt as though the file was calling me by name, urging me to click it. And stubbornly, I did. [[File:1769462135133.png|center|thumb|360x360px]] To my surprise, my antivirus software detected no malicious anomalies, so I began to read. The contents described the story of a man living within a cycle of habits that the author referred to as the “rat circle.” This cycle kept him stagnant, unable to “flow with the universe,” and claimed that through enlightenment one could “become one with God.” At the end of the text, there was an unsigned sentence that read: ''“If you practice equity, even if you die, you shall not perish.”'' At the time, I didn’t understand it. I didn’t know which “God” it referred to, nor had I ever heard anything about “flowing with the universe.” I decided to delete the file and continue with my work, completely forgetting about it. I finished my shift and headed home, and as I walked, the text resurfaced in my mind—not as something confusing, but as something reflective. ''Who was that man?'' ''Could I be that man?'' I asked myself—but chose to ignore the question for the moment. I considered that walk my moment of peace: a quiet interval where my mind could rest from workplace stress, and I didn’t want to ruin it. When I arrived home, I did everything I needed to do and prepared for bed. But as I lay down and relaxed, the question returned—this time with far greater intensity: ''Could I be that man? What have I built over the years?'' Uncomfortable as it was, I tried to evade it. Yet the more I avoided it, the more it spiraled through my thoughts. I fed myself shallow justifications that only made my lack of purpose more apparent: ''What benefits have I truly gained from the [[M.E.G.|M.E.G]]. that directly impact my life? Four years in this organization, and not even a “thank you.” Ungrateful, all of them. But I can’t afford to complain—after all, this gives me a roof over my head. I have no right to protest.'' '''I didn’t sleep at all that night.'''
----
Chapter II — No Blindness Is Worse Than That Which Refuses to See
My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. Days pass quickly, and nothing of interest ever happens, so I gradually begin to forget what occurred that night. I continue with my monotonous, draining routine until, on an otherwise ordinary day, something finally changes: a level known as '''Perpetual Stagnation''' is discovered. Making use of my free time, I decide to read the recently published article about this newly found level.
----
[[User:Backexp/Sandbox|←]] → [[User:Backexp/Sandbox2|↻]] [[Level List|⌂]]
=="Perpetual Stagnation"== {{Class Undetermined}} The '''Perpetual Stagnation''' level is an anomalous level of the Backrooms. ===Description=== ---- Perpetually Stagnant is a no-exit level characterized by an endless staircase with a structure similar to that of an old apartment building frozen in time. The staircase extends infinitely both upward and downward, leading to the conclusion that no edge will ever be foun...
''Bla bla bla bla bla bla bla bla bla.'' Nothing new. It felt as though all levels were the same, with only a handful that truly stood out. After this disappointment, I was about to return to my work when I noticed that, at the very bottom of the page, there was a document classified as unidentified, bearing the same title as the other file I had read days earlier. At that point, I thought someone was playing a joke on me, but I decided to open it anyway, as the question planted by the previous file resurfaced in my mind, and I held onto the hope of finding answers that might soften the harshness of reality. It did the exact opposite. Upon opening the document, a cold sentence awaited me: '''''Do you really plan to waste your entire life like this?''''' . .. ... Silence. An uncomfortable silence that felt endless. ''Who was reading my thoughts?'' ''Who was behind all of this?'' ''Was this some new mental conditioning program from the [[M.E.G.]]?'' But no matter how much I questioned it, I was fighting myself more than anything else, so I set my curiosity aside. In an attempt to distract my thoughts, I returned to work. The hours flew by, and then the days. Yet the sentence never left. In fact, it struck my mind even harder now, hammering at my skull like a carpenter pounding wood, mistaking my brain for timber. My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. My alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep. One day that was no longer just another day, a colleague named Stefania approached my desk. < ----
"Hey, are you okay? You’ve seemed really restless these past few days,” she asked. "Yeah, I’ve just been very stressed with work documents,” I replied. Somehow, one thing led to another, and we eventually began talking about the recently discovered level. “Hey, have you heard about the cult-like groups surrounding that level called '''Perpetual Stagnation?'''” At that moment, I felt my already disordered mind begin to spiral, yet I managed to remain composed and continue the conversation. "What do you mean?” I asked. "There are reports of people claiming the existence of little-known sects, located in remote areas, that dedicate their entire lives to worshipping this level,” she replied, sounding oddly excited. "Why? What’s so important about it" —By then, I had started to sweat. The stress and the revelation only made everything more confusing. "No one knows for sure,” she said, “but those who managed to return from their expeditions claim that these sects believe the level to be an illusory veil, and that there is an incomprehensible deity ruling over it. Crazy, right?" That was honestly all I needed to hear. I didn’t care about their culture or their explanations, so I cut the conversation short. “Yeah… well, I need to get back to work. Goodbye.” She replied with a confused farewell and walked away.
---- That information spun around in my head until I felt dizzy. I tried to keep my feet on the ground, but I failed miserably. At that moment, I was incapable of staying sane under the weight of everything I had to process. Then, spontaneously, a moment of clarity surfaced in my mind, as if I had briefly received help from an unknown force. It allowed me to ask myself: ''"Is that supposed god calling me?” “Should I go?” “What if it’s a trap?” “I can’t abandon my job."'' My mind could not comprehend what was happening. ''Why me? — I’m just a stupid and simple programmer. Why me...?'' Days pass, and my alarm goes off. I get up, have breakfast, get ready, go to work, work, return home, rest, and sleep... '''But now, it’s worse'''
----
Chapter III — When the Path Begins to Illuminate
On January 31st, 1999, I received an email from an unknown sender. Its subject was simple: “flow.”There was no content inside. By that point, paranoia had already taken hold of me. I began skipping work, feeling as though my lack of purpose was erasing everything I had built. Home was the only place where I felt safe. But in that moment of frustration, I realized that this “home” was not truly a home—it was a 7×7 cubic meter room. It felt more like a cage than a warm and comforting place. And with that realization came another: everything I had built was nothing more than an insignificant speck of dust to the M.E.G. ''What am I doing with my life? I asked myself as I cried uncontrollably on my bed.'' ''Why am I even here?'' For years, I had believed that I had built an identity—something authentic and my own—only to now realize that I had created nothing but a façade, one that concealed who I truly was. And although I only understood this now, it was already too late. With the passage of time, that identity had faded away, leaving behind nothing but fragmented memories of what once existed. Days passed, and my alarm no longer rang. I did not wake up. I did not eat breakfast. I did not get ready. I did not go to work. I did not work. I did not return home. I did not rest. I did not sleep. I stopped counting the days and lost all sense of time—but at least, I had '''escaped monotony.''' … '''I was fired.''' Yes, I lost my job—but at that point, I no longer cared. Still, a question crossed my mind: ''Why did I prioritize this existential crisis over my work?'' But it was already too late to question anything. I had nothing left to lose, and '''he''' was calling me. There was no longer any mental barrier separating me from Level '''Perpetually Stagnant.''' ---- Deciphering the entrances to this level was not particularly difficult—at least, not for me. After all, I was a cybersecurity specialist and a database analyst for the M.E.G. It didn’t take long to locate vulnerabilities in the system and identify the access points to Perpetually Stagnant. Besides, I was so insignificant to the M.E.G. that they never bothered to record the location of my residence. I would not have to deal with legal consequences later. ''“Find a door with achromatic colors in [[Level 909]], within the section known as ‘The House.’”'' Easy enough, I thought. After all, aside from being a cybersecurity expert, I was also an explorer dedicated to documenting dangerous levels. Reaching Level 909 posed no real challenge. ---- When I arrived, my mind was on the verge of collapse. My thoughts were completely scattered; I could not calm myself even for a moment. It felt as though my mind was being boiled, while my emotions clawed their way toward my throat. I was losing control—on the brink of breaking—until relief washed over my body. I found it. The door. The door to the level stood right before my eyes.
----
Chapter IV — Words That Please Are Not True; Words That Are True Do Not Please
I was deeply disappointed upon encountering this level. ''Is this what I abandoned my home for?'' I exclaimed. Being physically present here felt the same as when my fingertips touch the cold surface of my phone screen. There was nothing special about it, and that only deepened my frustration—because the complete absence of hope, after having once felt a promising connection, is far worse than never having hope at all. ''Is this really why I left my home?'' I exclaimed again. ''Maybe the sects were always wrong,'' I thought. ''Maybe I began all of this simply to give my life a sense of purpose. My subconscious already knew how deplorable my state was, and yet I chose to ignore it.'' I spoke and spoke, receiving no answer—no reply, not even an echo. I climbed and climbed, finding nothing different, only more cold, dirty steps. ''I won’t be here much longer,'' I kept telling myself.
Chapter V Chapter VI Chapter M Chapter MxM Chapter ∞
Centuries passed, then millennia, then eons… or at least, from my perspective. I had no idea how much time had passed; I only knew that I knew nothing about myself anymore. How stubborn I had been. The worst part was that nothing had changed—and that prevented my soul from screaming in hope, from being heard, from being rescued. The immutable staircase had become a reflection of my depressed self: continuous, endless, yet never changing. I could feel the void seeping into my being. But then—'''something happened.''' I remembered my life before entering this level. It was a fleeting memory, but enough to spark something within me. I once would have thought everything I had built was worthless had I continued living that old life, but now I recalled it as a fragmented, warm, comforting thought—something that brought me peace, like the feeling of walking home after work. And then I realized it. What I had been searching for was not meaning in my life. Viewed from the outside, I wasn’t seeking identity, but recognition; I was constantly chasing new things without appreciating what I already had. I wasn’t seeking understanding—I wanted to be seen. When I spoke with Stefania, my heart raced not because of the level she mentioned, but because, for a moment, I did not feel invisible. '''And so I began to understand.''' This level is not depressing—it is neutral, from another perspective. It is not hopeless—it is welcoming. It is not dark and cold to frighten you; it is dark and cold because it is empty, so that you may release everything you carry within you and reconfigure your being. And then I understood that even if I do not have all the answers right now, I can choose between running after others—or flowing harmoniously with the universe.
And as I spoke those words, the universe manifested.
[[File:Yin-and-yang.gif|center|thumb]]
}} ---- {{Collapse|num=back2|final=>Close attachment 02|initial=>Open attachment 02|body=
Chapter Ω — The beginning of the end The True Level
Natural Becoming
{{Class Habitable|safety=Eternal Harmony|security=The Origin and the End of All Things|entity= Manifestation of the Tao}} The Natural Becoming represents the true nature of all things. ==Description== ---- '''Natural Becoming''' is characterized by a gigantic mountain surrounded by an infinite, extremely dense forest. The temperature remains at a constant 22°C, and humidity fluctuates between 40% and 60%, creating a consistently pleasant environment. Within this level, fatigue is never felt; however, sleep remains possible at will. There is no need to eat, drink water, or fulfill basic hygienic needs.Natural Becoming is composed of two zones: the Stairway (楼梯) and the Temple (圣殿).
----
==
The Stairway (楼梯)
== ---- The Stairway is the first structure encountered upon arrival and is the only accessible route leading to the Temple. It consists of countless stone steps—so many that reaching the summit may take several hours. Beyond this ascent and the breathtaking views of the forest below, there is nothing else of particular relevance to note regarding the Stairway. [[File:File 00000000011471f6a63f2fd7a597b04a.png|border|center|frameless|An artistic representation of the staircase.]] ==
The Temple (圣殿) — Exterior
== ---- The Temple is the most prominent feature of this level. When viewed from the outside, it bears similarities to the Dongyue Temple in China, with the distinction that it is almost entirely composed of jade and gold, while the ground is made of silver and iron. Standing outside the Temple are three massive statues crafted entirely from cinnabar, each representing a figure of great significance within Taoism. [[File:File 0000000000d071f6b0398214e7f74c89.png|border|center|360x360px|frameless|An artistic representation of the temple.]] ----
  • Statue 1
  • Statue 2
  • Statue 3
The first statue represents the Great Trinity of the Three Pure Ones (三清), with an inscription engraved into the cinnabar reading: ''“Heavenly Lords of the Primordial Beginning and the Spiritual Treasure.”'' [[File:InShot 20260123 184331412.gif|center|thumb|480x480px]] ----
The Great Trinity of the Three Pure Ones (三清)
The second statue depicts the Eight Immortals (八仙), with an inscription engraved into the precious stone that reads: ''“The Eight Geniuses.”'' [[File:InShot 20260123 192525410.jpg|center|thumb|480x480px]] ----
The Eight Immortals (八仙)
The third statue portrays the Jade Emperor (玉皇大帝), bearing the inscription: ''“Supreme Ruler of Heaven.”'' [[File:InShot 20260123 191150802.jpg|center|thumb|480x480px]] ----
The Great Jade Emperor (玉皇大帝)
---- ==
The Temple (圣殿) — Interior
== ---- Inside, the Temple retains the same material composition as its exterior, with the sole difference that the floor is made entirely of pure gold. At the center stands a jade table upon which rests a scroll crafted from an unknown material. Parallel to this table are two massive stones positioned opposite one another. Each stone bears its respective symbol and text, describing Taiji (太极) and Wuji (无极)—the first two fundamental forces. In front of the table stands a golden pillar bearing a symbol and text describing Yin–Yang (阴阳), the third and final fundamental force. [[File:Images (1) (14).jpg|center|thumb|540x480px]]
----
Wuji (无极) ---- Wuji is the silent vastness that precedes form. In the beginning, there exists only an immense void—the womb of all seeds, a space without contours where time has not yet been born. This primordial state, without limits or dualities, is the invisible matrix of the universe. It is not an empty nothingness, but rather the hidden fullness of reality itself: the stillness where unborn stars dream and future forests rest unseen. Within Wuji, every possibility exists veiled like a seed in darkness, for in that infinite silence lies the slumbering promise of every world yet to come. Taiji (太极) ---- In Taiji, the universe takes its first turn. Here, stillness and energy coexist, yin and yang entwined in their initial dance. From this impulse arises the pulse of the world—a rhythm that separates light from shadow, warmth from cold, ascent from descent. Taiji is the great wheel that propels the heavens, where stillness gives birth to motion and motion inevitably returns to rest. At the center of this dynamic balance beats the seed of all existence. Ying-Yang (阴阳) ---- Yin and Yang are the eternal dance of two opposing forces that complete one another. Each exists through the other, bound in an embrace that sustains the balance of the world. Where there is light, shadow is born; where there is action, stillness flourishes. Within the seed of every day lies the essence of night, and within the heart of every night already shines the seed of day. Thus, clarity and darkness alternate endlessly in an infinite cycle, neither existing without the other. Each step of this natural dance weaves the profound harmony that upholds all life.
☼ ☼ ☼
----
Each monument is embedded with numerous jade gemstones, though Wuji possesses notably more than the others, followed by Taiji, and lastly Yin–Yang, symbolically representing the hierarchy of the three fundamental states. As one approaches the center, an invisible aura can be felt enveloping the body. This aura relaxes the muscles and brings the mind into a state of focused serenity, as if on the verge of meditation. At the very center lies the scroll; upon opening it, nothing can be found except a material resembling gold, radiating a dazzling brilliance and a perfection beyond words. After reading the contents of the scroll, the surrounding environment will change immediately and abruptly, erasing all structures present in the area. Everything will vanish without any form of transition. What remains is an infinite void, where one may ultimately encounter the Creator.
[[File:Forgotten photo.png|center|thumb|540x540px]]
''When you gaze into the abyss, the abyss gazes back at you.'' [[File:Lost narrative.png|center|thumb|540x540px]] ''When history is heard...
''Even the greatest tragic narrative begins to fragment within wu wei.''
''Huh? Where am I? Just moments ago, I was inside that strange temple…'' I watched as my soul separated from my body, and with it, as my consciousness divided from my subconscious— my self drifting away from my material self... Even so, I felt an indescribable calm, for I knew that, in essence, I had never abandoned it, and it had never abandoned me— not even without the need to seek it. Chapter ☯ — The Tao (道) ''And then I perceived it, yet there was nothing that could be seen.'' ''It exists before all distinction, not as something that is, nor as something that is not.'' ''It has no form, yet all form arises within it. It does not act, yet nothing occurs without its presence.'' ''It is unity without a center, a source without origin, stillness that does not need to move for everything else to move.'' ''Before being and non-being there was fullness, a void so complete that nothing was missing from it.'' ''From that void, nothing was born, but the possibility of being born. It was not creation— it was manifestation.'' ''The unity reflected itself, and polarity appeared, not as a fracture, but as a rhythm.'' ''Thus arose expansion and return, light and shadow, the yin that receives and the yang that impels.'' ''They are not opposites; they are gestures of the same flow.'' ''When movement comes to rest, it does not vanish: it returns to the origin.'' ''When form dissolves, it is not lost: it returns to emptiness.'' ''Here, being does not assert itself, and non-being does not deny. Both partake in that which cannot be grasped.'' ''Nothing is separate, nothing ascends, nothing falls.'' ''For there was never any distance between the source and what is manifested.'' ''Everything is a reflection, yet there is no mirror.'' ''Everything is a path, yet there is no destination.'' ''And then I understood'' ''that the Tao that can be named is not the true Tao.''
{{Collapse|num=realtao|final=|initial=[[File:Tao lse.png|center|thumb|360x360px]]|body= ----
If you have made it this far, perhaps something within you was listening. If the protagonist, Stefan Kronecker, was able to move forward and reach illumination even as his world was falling apart, then perhaps we can as well. Maybe not with the help of the Tao, but with the help of a friend, a family member, or even a complete stranger. We are all capable of moving forward and reaching our own form of illumination. Nothing ends here. Nothing begins. It simply flows. Remember: every story has a happy ending. And if you are not happy yet, it means we have not reached the end. Thank you for reading
